document: Introduction to Internet of Things Assignment-Week 10 Solutions

questions.explanation: Smart city is an urban system. (Please refer Lecture 46@8:02)

questions.questionText: Which of the following is an area of focus in smart parking lots?
options: c. Detection of slots in parking lots
questions.explanation: Auto-routing, auto-charging and slot detection are focus areas in smart parking. (Please refer Lecture 46@19:42)

questions.questionText:
Select the statement(s) that are true for smart health.
Statement I: Smart health is low-cost and portable.
Statement II: Smart health allows remote check-ups and diagnosis.
Statement III: Smart health enables effortless health monitoring.
options: c. Statements I, II, and III
questions.explanation: Smart health is low-cost/portable, supports remote check-ups & diagnosis, and enables effortless monitoring. (Please refer Lecture 46@26:12)

questions.questionText: What refers to combining information from multiple sensor sources?
questions.explanation: Multi-sensor data fusion combines information from multiple sensor sources. (Please refer Lecture 47@6:52)

questions.questionText: The ensemble of all the decisions happens at the _______ level (in data fusion).
questions.explanation: The ensemble of multiple decisions occurs at the decision level. (Please refer Lecture 47@11:10)

questions.questionText: The smart parking system reduces traffic _______
questions.explanation: Smart parking reduces traffic congestion. (Please refer Lecture 47@16:58)

questions.questionText: DLNA (used in HAN standards) expands to:
questions.explanation: DLNA stands for Digital Living Network Alliance. (Please refer Lecture 48@13:49)

questions.questionText: Smart home infrastructure cannot include wireless devices.
questions.explanation: Smart home infrastructure may include wired and/or wireless devices. (Please refer Lecture 48@6:17)

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are layers of Zigbee?
questions.explanation: Zigbee layers include Physical, MAC and Network layers. (Please refer Lecture 48@16:23)

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a property of V2X?
options: a. Restricted to a single source
questions.explanation: V2X follows a distributed architecture. (Please refer Lecture 49@12:06)

questions.questionText: CCN is derived from ICN architecture.
questions.explanation: CCN is derived from Information Centric Networking (ICN). (Please refer Lecture 49@15:32)

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank. In Vehicle-to-human/human-to-vehicle interaction, the human communicating with the vehicle _______
options: a. is present in another vehicle
b. is present in the same vehicle
c. is present outside the vehicle on the roadside
questions.explanation: In V2H/H2V, the human is present outside the vehicle on the roadside. (Please refer Lecture 49@3:13)

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank. ICV communication is based on _______
questions.explanation: ICV is based on Dedicated Short Range Communication (DSRC). (Please refer Lecture 50@7:22)

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are domains of VANET?
questions.explanation: In-vehicle and Ad-hoc are domains of VANET. (Please refer Lecture 50@14:36)

questions.questionText: The vehicles cannot be mobile in the Ad-hoc domain of VANET.
questions.explanation: Vehicles are mobile in the Ad-hoc domain of VANET. (Please refer Lecture 50@16:59)
